message:
feat(core): implement foundational types for terminal UI rendering

This commit adds the core type system that forms the foundation of
OpenTUI, implementing RGBA colors with Porter-Duff blending, text
styling with attributes, and terminal cells with grapheme support.

src/color.rs - RGBA Color System:
- Rgba struct with f32 components (r, g, b, a) in [0.0, 1.0] range
- Porter-Duff "over" compositing: blend_over() with proper premultiplication
  Formula: result.rgb = src.rgb*src.a + dst.rgb*dst.a*(1-src.a)
           result.a = src.a + dst.a*(1-src.a)
- Color creation: new(), rgb(), from_rgb_u8(), from_hex(), from_hsv()
- Hex parsing supports #RGB, #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA formats
- HSV conversion for hue-based color manipulation
- Alpha operations: with_alpha(), multiply_alpha()
- Color interpolation: lerp() for smooth transitions
- Conversion: to_rgb_u8() for ANSI output
- Color mode mapping: to_256_color(), to_16_color() for terminal compat
- Predefined constants: BLACK, WHITE, RED, GREEN, BLUE, etc.
- Comprehensive test suite: 25 tests for blending, parsing, conversion

src/style.rs - Text Styling:
- TextAttributes bitflags (u8): BOLD, DIM, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, BLINK,
  INVERSE, HIDDEN, STRIKETHROUGH
- Style struct with optional fg/bg colors, attributes, link_id
- Builder pattern: Style::builder().fg(color).bold().build()
- Style merging: merge() combines styles with precedence
- Default style uses terminal colors (None for fg/bg)

src/cell.rs - Terminal Cell:
- CellContent enum for flexible character storage:
  - Char(char): Single Unicode codepoint (most ASCII/Latin)
  - Grapheme(Arc<str>): Complex grapheme clusters (emoji, ZWJ sequences)
  - Empty: Cleared cell
  - Continuation: Second cell of wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- Cell struct: content + fg + bg + attributes + link_id
- Cell operations: new(), from_grapheme(), clear(), continuation()
- Alpha blending: blend_over() composes cells with transparency
- Opacity support: blend_with_opacity() multiplies alpha channel
- Display width awareness for proper terminal rendering

src/error.rs - Error Handling:
- Error enum with variants: Io, InvalidInput, TerminalNotAvailable
- Transparent Display/Debug via thiserror-like impl
- Result<T> type alias for Result<T, Error>
- std::error::Error trait implementation

src/event.rs - Event Infrastructure:
- Event enum for callback dispatch
- LogLevel enum: Debug, Info, Warn, Error
- Global callbacks: set_event_callback(), set_log_callback()
- emit_event() and emit_log() for event propagation
- Thread-safe via static OnceLock storage

src/link.rs - Hyperlink Pool:
- LinkPool manages OSC 8 hyperlink IDs
- URL storage with unique ID generation
- get_or_create() for deduplication
- get_url() for ID lookup
- Used by renderer for terminal hyperlinks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
